{{Quote|The taku forests of Telkadis are home to a particularly devious predator known as the Telkadis hiding tree spider.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Telkadis''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="Creatures" /> located in the [[Telkadis system/Legends|Telkadis system]], a part of the [[Greater Plooriod sector/Legends|Greater Plooriod sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] portion of<ref name="NorthernDependencies" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Its surface featured [[Forest/Legends|forests]] of [[taku]] [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The area was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Rodent/Legends|rodent]] known as the [[volete]] as well as the [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] [[Spider/Legends|arachnid]] known as the [[Telkadis hiding tree spider]]. The latter burrowed into the taku trees, from which they ambushed prey such as voletes and also attacked Telkadian foresters and [[logger]]s when their host trees were threatened. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Telkadis was mentioned in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of<ref name="Creatures" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Chandrila">{{CSWECite|I|119|Chandrila}}</ref> [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the Telkadis hiding tree spider.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Telkadis was mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the celestial body was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Telkadis [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Telkadis itself, in grid square M-7.<ref name="EAOC" />
